textByHash.a668e19888ba289065275a35e6990c6bb13a972b: You found me! What a day, huh, Humpy Plumpy?
textByHash.bdb54c8ad0652523fee2ba4da6aadd17b5ce215f: Yes, yes... In fact, I've been getting rather used to life out here.
textByHash.9cf3fcb1765a90569e85210ea7a5efe8b505cb60: Oh, I'm Alan. I was just visiting the area as a tourist, but I ended up straying from the pack a little.
textByHash.e24b7354ade2e682ff525c70ae3de3ab61286554: Oh, no thank you. Like I say, I've actually come to quite like it here. Had I never got lost, I might never have met my best friend, Numpty Bumpty.
textByHash.45b0042c18d78311268b0ca9a63d6efbd2018a34: Still, it is nice for us to finally have a guest. In fact, you should stay for a while! I'll prepare a little feast for us all! What do you say?
textByHash.606ff1887ab536b133716eece629ac44f86bf5a6: Wonderful! Would you mind staying here while I go out and gather the food? I may not have known you long, but I'm sure I can trust you to keep an eye on Wumty Scrumpty over there.
textByHash.11a8271eab6a3b37389e850d18c986c16a2391c9: Right, time for me to get going. Don't worry, Hampty, our new friend here is going to take good care of you while I'm gone. Love you lots. Miss you already.
textByHash.21a2e54e8f4aed17e32b08411cfcbc2f335b7c2f: Okay, I'll wait until you return. Just don't go getting lost out there!
textByHash.4541cbf46da87003d85354a03365fa922851b0ae: I'm back! How was Plumper? He didn't cause you any problems, did he?
textByHash.a38f3fcdeca85126a96b81afdd4d4fb6536fe11e: Wonderful! I'm so glad to hear it. Now, I've got quite the meal lined up for us. Would you mind giving me a hand bringing everything inside?
textByHash.4b9b61921d399d9ff393d230a0079f4d9888d0b1: You know, friend, it wasn't until now that I came to realise that I'd actually been lost for a long time. I had a job back home... I had a family... and yet, something was missing.
textByHash.c496344f72f0017581bfa7a1677abfa9c5a90815: But now... now I think I've finally found myself. For the first time in my life, I know who I am... who I'm meant to be. I think this can be a proper home. A home for me, and for Rumpy as well. A forever home.
textByHash.c93ebe4afb9eb597d7874446347807263bc97ccc: I might - which patch were you thinking of?
textByHash.31695811dd0e28f6544fc14e980339fe93649a20: You don't have any seeds planted in that patch. Plant some and I might agree to look after it for you.
textByHash.c13ac83f4a7fc23480d36a92e5d6a55228724aef: That patch is diseased - you should cure it before asking me to be responsible for it!
textByHash.aa74f7c8cf0dd5cd9836ea178e223bbc22d87cec: That patch is dead - it's too late for me to do anything about it now.
textByHash.afa4c7dd799e237ef8992f1ef147eb9959379c1e: That patch is already fully grown! I don't know what you want me to do with it!
textByHash.17a7874b3e46e001b95d1350f6f02f713d319256: I'm already looking after that patch for you.
textByHash.4055062736893ed3b8abc7c54a8e4f847f4c734a: If you like, but I want for that.
textByHash.3f11dbf93226711b5819390774f4c4bb733e9f18: Well, I'm not wasting my time for free.
textByHash.60f719b1a5f6ab656f8f0b0f2606e28ff1e6e430: That'll do nicely, sir. Leave it with me - I'll make sure that patch grows for you.
textByHash.9d1b16fbbba1a8e9b65bf6fee6778793fca73a2f: That'll do nicely, madam. Leave it with me - I'll make sure that patch grows for you.
textByHash.5b80d2f6864a3004e86352a71a2a922dbff854e2: That'll do nicely. Leave it with me - I'll make sure that patch grows for you.
textByHash.f6ffd8c9fcb2ce2979be99395bca02aefc31d32e: Poison ivy bushes don't get diseased, so you needn't worry about having it protected.
textByHash.a6edaedac0ebb731b3bb6159665e00c5cb4113ef: You can put up to ten potatoes, cabbages or onions in vegetable sacks, although you can't have a mix in the same sack.
textByHash.55311f0bfe322285664c0c9f55e1c4011b33b9e4: You can put up to five tomatoes, strawberries, apples, bananas or oranges into a fruit basket, although you can't have a mix in the same basket.
textByHash.aeb9f67ee8999d8f166f56091580bd6dd9bc95eb: You can buy all the farming tools from farming shops, which can be found close to the allotments.
textByHash.82b3bb051e232dbf89fa6df668611f47d4ad2bb4: You can fill plantpots with soil from any empty patch, if you have a gardening trowel.
textByHash.ae656597b0ced63456f873ed91659b56014f23e4: Tree seeds must be grown in a plantpot of soil into a sapling, and then transferred to a tree patch to continue growing to adulthood.
textByHash.20e3f04e5791785d37c8525109dc22537f5e59ba: If you want to make your own sacks and baskets you'll need to use the loom that's near the Farming shop in Falador. If you're a good enough craftswoman, that is.
textByHash.d46691d570a70f6a1d88c2fec06b6e861e94c541: If you want to make your own sacks and baskets you'll need to use the loom that's near the Farming shop in Falador. If you're a good enough craftsman, that is.
textByHash.5a5f6990cbed15a5951025baa60d4864c8dc4455: You don't have to buy all your plantpots you know, you can make them yourself on a pottery wheel. If you're a good enough craftswoman, that is.
textByHash.28808ca5eda526554b0caa99c08e7f545b26b0f3: You don't have to buy all your plantpots you know, you can make them yourself on a pottery wheel. If you're a good enough craftsman, that is.
textByHash.0e23b2ce623239ece1296cca620e077ffe67cf10: Don't just throw away your weeds after you've raked a patch - put them in a compost bin and make some compost.
textByHash.bdb606ebc97dab841a6849b38e89579be3605829: Applying compost to a patch will not only reduce the chance that your crops will get diseased, but you will also grow more crops to harvest.
textByHash.2b5080fa3126d7f92fc6d4d7342716184af27023: Supercompost is far better than normal compost, but more expensive to make. You need to rot the right type of item; show me an item, and I'll tell you if it's super- compostable or not.
textByHash.89b3d09817bb8064531176f780ffdeb6fb5e6a03: Vegetables, hops and flowers need constant watering - if you ignore my advice, you will sooner or later find yourself in possession of a dead farming patch.
textByHash.a948ca937c898af560bec30046158b9876f416f8: The only way to cure a bush or tree of disease is to prune away the diseased leaves with a pair of secateurs. For all other crops I would just apply some plant-cure.
textByHash.187dd647e8ad77d59c8e69788ea44ca397ab2f05: There are five other Farming areas like this one - Elstan's area near Falador, Dantaera's area near Catherby, Kragen's area near Ardougne, Lyra's area in Morytania, Marisi's area in Hosidius and Oswallt's area
textByHash.54045b3bbdd54aa75f5e896632323b9c1643a738: If you want to grow fruit trees you could try a few places: Catherby, Brimhaven, Gnome Stronghold and the Farming Guild all have fruit tree patches.
textByHash.532c2f53eeb1eee0e9c290a18fd1128a373a915e: Hops are good for brewing ales. I believe there's a brewery up in Keldagrim somewhere, and I've heard rumours that a place called Phasmatys used to be good for that type of thing. 'Fore they all died, of course.
textByHash.a29a52df2fc06db96d8448aaded4561eb6c73210: Bittercap mushrooms can only be grown in a special patch in Morytania, near the Mort Myre swamp. There the ground is especially dank and suited to growing poisonous fungii.
textByHash.5f5d70ffca0e626c6fd81ee864018c689f4978a1: There is a special patch for growing Belladonna - I believe that it is somewhere near Draynor Manor, where the ground is a tad 'unblessed'.
textByHash.1fc91822a6dd38a1807d1ec0d6aa54c61e2e9529: That depends on whether I have it to sell. What is it that you're looking for?
textByHash.a51cdf72f0b2b8b5e851da91043ac299caa1aa82: Plant cure, eh? I might have some put aside for myself. Tell you what, I'll sell you some plant cure for 25 coins if you like.
textByHash.f396d0e2f6f397730e17adfd3e54f96ff74fb88b: A bucket of compost, eh? I might have one spare... tell you what, I'll sell it to you for 35 coins if you like.
textByHash.b2941d6dc55521fcde70a73e96ac545bea943cb7: A rake, eh? I might have one spare... tell you what, I'll sell it to you for 15 coins if you like.
textByHash.f4690b47fb36f6e8062ceecf917120be8ec67891: Eh, I suppose I can spare you a plant pot, but I'll want 40 coins for it.
textByHash.aabe0b6c657fbbf792905b4ed2fb1cc30adf585d: A watering can, eh? I might have one spare... tell you what, I'll sell it to you for 25 coins if you like.
textByHash.1484caf97dc54f48c63e7eee46d6fa1c9b425565: A gardening trowel, eh? I might have one spare... tell you what, I'll sell it to you for 15 coins if you like.
textByHash.6375c9acc18ebc3accc9f2430bd53e11697376a5: A seed dibber, eh? I might have one spare... tell you what, I'll sell it to you for 15 coins if you like.
textByHash.ccdf42e3786605948793f0e0cf72b2cd41449346: Which patch would you like to pay protection for?
